summary: DeVault provides his July 2021 status update while relocating to Amsterdam. The post covers progress on his programming language project (Hare), including a codegen rewrite, new math operations, DNS resolution, and POSIX utility implementations. He notes the aerc email client needs a new maintainer after Reto Brunner stepped down. SourceHut work has focused on the GraphQL API for lists.sr.ht, though the move abroad has slowed some deliverables.
key_points: The Hare codegen rewrite is underway and looking much better than its predecessor
New contributors are adding floating-point math operations and f32-to-string conversion to Hare
DeVault implemented DNS resolution and a 'dial' function, and wrote POSIX utilities in Hare as practical demonstrations
The aerc email client maintainer has stepped down, and a replacement is being sought
SourceHut's lists.sr.ht GraphQL API is substantially progressed but delayed by the international move
money_quotes: We've broken ground on the codegen rewrite, and it's looking much better than its predecessor.
I also started writing some POSIX utilities in the new language for fun.
We're still looking for someone to contribute in cryptography, and in date/time support.
I have mostly been focused on writing the GraphQL API for lists.sr.ht.
key_insight: Hare is maturing rapidly with community contributions in math, networking, and codegen, while DeVault balances language development with SourceHut infrastructure work during an international relocation.
